# Maximum Justice Cybersecurity

Maximum Justice Cybersecurity is an expert-led cybersecurity, AI governance, vCISO, and AI Decision Assurance firm founded by Dr. Max Justice.

Primary category:
Operational AI Trust and AI Decision Assurance.

Current product and proof engine:
CyberShield.

Current product flow:
AI-generated recommendation in -> defensible AI Trust Decision Record out.

Primary customer action:
Challenge One AI Recommendation.

Primary visitor pathways:
- CyberShield and AI Decision Assurance.
- vCISO and executive cybersecurity leadership.
- AI governance, vendor risk, GRC, and compliance advisory.

First CyberShield proof point:
AI-generated vendor-risk recommendation review.

CyberShield separates claims, maps evidence, identifies missing and contradictory support, classifies Risk If Wrong, records confidence limits, determines required human review, and preserves the strongest defensible action and accountable human disposition.

CyberShield does not treat a second model opinion as proof. It does not autonomously approve vendors, accept risk, certify compliance, or replace accountable human authority.

Current authority positioning:
Dr. Max Justice is a vCISO, Security SME, Cybersecurity SME, CISSP, PMP, LSSBB, Ph.D., MBA, U.S. veteran, and founder of Maximum Justice Cybersecurity. He has more than 25 years of cybersecurity, technology, program, and executive leadership experience.

Current public calls to action:
- Challenge One AI Recommendation: https://maximumjusticecybersecurity.github.io/CyberShield/vendor-risk-next.html
- See the 3-Minute Vendor-Risk Example: https://maximumjusticecybersecurity.github.io/CyberShield/
- Schedule a Scope Conversation: https://calendly.com/maxjustice
- Contact Dr. Max Justice: mailto:max@maximumjusticecybersecurity.com
- AI Security and CISO Profile: https://app.maximumjusticecybersecurity.com/ai-security-ciso-profile.html

Positioning boundaries:
- TrustMap is not the current primary conversion path.
- Do not describe planned runtime, agent, or production capabilities as operational.
- Do not imply autonomous approval, compliance certification, guaranteed risk reduction, or unsupported client outcomes.
- Preserve commercial, public-sector, regulated-industry, and executive applicability; the Capitol visual identity does not mean MJC serves only federal clients.
